chunk_id: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)_p7
text:
PROLEGOMENA

The Omnipotent and all-pervading Gayatri, Ganesa and
the Grahas have all been exceedingly merciful in enabling us
to place before the reading public this monumental treatise,
SARAVALI, with my English rendering and commentary.

Kalyana Varma, the famous author of this famous work
was a king himself ruling a place called Vyaghrapada, which
place some of us trace in modern Madhya Pradesh and yet
some in modern Gujarat. He is also identified as Karna Deva,
He must have flourished before the 10th century as Bhattotpala
(966 A.D.) reverentially referred to our illustrious author in
his various commentaries on works like Brihat Jataka, Brihat
Samhita etc. A statement akin to our author’s date as mid-
13th century is, however, incredible. Seeing Bhattotpala’s
reference and statements by various writers, our author seemed
to belong tothe 6th century, that is immediately after the
Great Master, Varaha Mihira.

During the time of Kalyana Varma, there were only a
few literary gems on astrology, viz. Parasara Hora, Brihat
Jataka, Hora Sara etc. Our author feels the limitation of
Brihat Jataka (vide sloka 2, ch 1 infra) and has taken the
quintessence of Maharshi Parasara’s Hora Sastra, apart from
other gteat works like Brihat Jataka, as could be seen in
chapters like ‘Nabhasa Yogas’. The Greek pundits, Yavana-
charyas, were more oftep acknowledged by him, his bold
assertion of his rejecting their superfluous doctrines, being
significant (vide sloka 4 ch 1). He further remains in gratitude
to many a progenitor like Satya, Badarayana (simultaneously
the author of Brahma Sutras), Chanakya, Chudamani, Hari,
Maniththa, Brahma Saunda etc.

As the reader progressess through the work, he is sure
to feel astrologically enlightened and find an invisible preceptor
—a new source of guidance in Kalyana Varma’s words which
bequeath to usthe unparallel and unsurpassed wisdom of
the sages.
book_name: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)

chunk_id: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)_p8
text:
4

The author’s vast study and scientific treatment of the
subject, able exposition and inimitable style will be evident
from the provided guidelines. Apart from being an astrologer
of high latitude, the king exhibits his literary aptitude in the
Janguage of the Gods, i.e. Sanscrit, through many slokas. He
was adept in traditional medicine also, vide ch. 11, dealing
with combinations to counteract lunar evils. Not only this,
he had reasonable knowledge of Yoga for Body Control, as he
refers to Basti Kriya (in ch. 11, sloka 4). (Basti Kriyais a
process of yogic cleaning of the lower intestines to strengthen
abdominal muscles and cure digestive and urinary disorders
and chronic constipation.)

Most later authors summarized or verbatim borrowed the
slokas of ourauthor. Mentionable among these are: Bala-
bhadra of Hora Ratna, Vaidyanatha of Jataka Parijata and
Mantreswara of Phala Deepika apart from the author of
Jataka Saradeepa. The two works, Hora Ratna, and Jataka
Saradeepa (the latter published from Tanjore in Sanscrit),
owe alot to Saravali.

Many important aspects of predictive astrology have
been well-explained by the author in the following pages.
Apart from ordinary preliminary information, the chapters on
Arishtas (evils) and their cancellation, combinations for
Renunciation, Nabhasa Yogas, planetary conjunction efc. may
prove ‘useful in delineation of horoscopes scientifically and
purposefully. Special mention need be made of chapters 22
through 26 which deal with effects of planets posited in various
signs. These positions stand to give modified effects if aspected
by other planets, clues to which effect have been given by the
author simultaneously. In these chapters, one finds aspects
between the Sun-Venus-Mercury mutually, in contradiction to
known principles. The author himself mentions about the
usual aspects in ch. 4, slokas 32-33. Apparently the said
aspects among Sun-Venus-Mercury are only meant to be
partial. Similar aspects continue in chapters 27-29. These dis-
cussions could be found in Hora Ratna and Jataka Saradeepa
also. As partial aspects are possible between the Sun, Mercury
and Venus mutually, one can infer that Kalyana Varma 7९७६
book_name: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)

chunk_id: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)_p9
text:
5
to limited orbs only, in the relevant context. Full aspects
cannot be extended between these planets.

Extensive and useful commentary with notes and quota-
tions from a large number of texts in Sanscrit, Tamil, Telugu,
Hindi and English have been added by me in appropriate
contexts. Even the most difficult aspects have been lucidly
and minutely explained for the benefit of all concerned, accord-
ing to my mite. Though maximum efforts have been made
to keep this long-awaited treatise rid of longueurs,; possibly
errors could have found their way into the text. Wecan only
seek the broadminded readers to view our short-comings
leniently.

The Publishers, Ranjan Publications, have fulfilled
the task of this treatise seeing its print in English for the first
time in a commendable way and with their usual missionary
enthusiasm. By this act they earn the gratitude of many
anxious readers of this divine science.

We now goto thetext praying Goddess Saraswati to
bestow us the required ‘digestive power’.

New Delhi Rj SANTHANAM
May 10, 1983

text:
6

AN EPITOME OF ASTROLOGICAL NADIS

By R. Santhanam

This is a prosilient work based on many golden principles
from Nadi Treatises, brought out for the first time in English.
The reader should not mistake this for the general individual
readings by Nadi astrologers. Numerous aphorisms are given
in this text as culled out from relevant treatises, viz. Satya
Nadi, Bhrigu Nandi Nadi, Bhujanga Nadi etc.

There are many attempts, in unique and hitherto un-
published lines in this work. For example, Rahu’s sign of fall
is denoted, nay declared, as Scorpio and it is stated that if the
Moon joins Rahu in this sign, a Libran will be distressed and
dullwitted. In view of the fact that Ayanamsa is the
main key to all the principles in this text, one sign
having been made into 150 equal divisions, the author,
Sri Santhanam, has ably and convincingly solved the
vexed precisional problem and the reader is rightly
guided to use these principles with the right Ayanamsa,
It is hoped that this work will be very warmly received by the
astrological fraternity.

THE SECRETS OF ASHTAKAVARGA

By R. Santhanam

An unassumingly original treatise compiled by Acharya
Mukunda Daivagna of Gharwal in Sanscrit, during his Ashram
life. His work NASHTA JATAKAM with Mr. Santhanam’s
English translation is already familiar to the readers. Our
present attempt is to expand the availability of Mukunda’s
magnificent treatises to one and all, Mukunda Daivagna was
none other than Mukunda Rama, the illustrious commentator
of Prasna Gnana, under the name of Aryasapthati. Reference
to Aryasapthati with reverence could be found in late Sri
V.Subramanya Sastri’s preface to his English translation of
Prasna Gnana,
book_name: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)

chunk_id: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)_p11
text:
7

The work in original has eleven chapters in 455 Devanagari
verses. To mention captions of chapters, some are : Graha and
Bhava Janyashtaka Vargas, Ashtaka Varga Dasa, Ashtaka
Vargarishta etc. The original has been constructed with addi-
tional help from Deva Keralam, Viswanatha Kavi, Brahma
Yaamala, Prithu Yasas, Karana (करण) Prakasa, Kalyana Varma,
Jaganmohana, Devasala etc.

The translator who christened the work as above,
expanded the work further so that a majority of pastmasters
are put together to express their views in one single work.
Reliable quotations and scholarly commentaries adore this
rich work. Hereis a golden opportunity for the lovers of
Hindu Astrology, particularly those who enjoy the Ashtaka-
varga method of prognastication for natal and transit charts.
This work in a way is a propylaeum of astrology.

DOCTRINES OF SUKA NADI

By R. Santhanam

An excellent and rare work containing principles on
predictive astrology. Exclusive aphorisms from authoritative
treatises on Hindu Astrology fully enrich this work. To
cite an instance : If the lords of the 5th and the 8th joinin the
Tth along with Saturn and Rahu, the female concerned does not
attain puberty at all. In another context, it is stated that if a
movable Rasi ascend containing the 8th lord therein, the native
enjoys full span of life. These are specific and exceptional
rules and the author has explained the rationale behind such
principles.

This work touches all houses including clues to time
events, principles covering marital match and such other
aspects. An indispensible work to those who breathe astrology
at all times
book_name: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)

chunk_id: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)_p12
text:
8
BHRIGU SUTRAM

Just as the celestial bodies represent the Almighty so
does a sage in many ways. Bhrigu Rishi with his divine insight,
not fruitfully possessed by an ordinary immortal, created this
work in simple Sutras. The Sanskrit principles by the sage
are not only astrologically rich but are very terse and are
capable of being committed to even ordinary memory. The
advantage; these rules pregnant of useful meanings can be at
one’s back and call.

Dealt with by the sage in the text are : the results of
various planets in each bhava along with many yogas. The
English translation includes detailed comments, the technique
of application and correlated views of other classicals.

This work abridges the lacuna in predictive astrology.

BRIHAT PARASARA HORA

OF SAGE PARASARA
(Sanskrit Verses with English translation)
This isa great traditional treatise on eastern astrology.
The science of astrology in the east took its incarnation

through the divine insight of Parasara, the Father of Hindu
Astrology.

The present English version is the first ever attempt in
the press of the world. To highlight briefly, the work deals
with basic aphorisms of Hindu astrology extending to difficult
and minute sides of the subject. Aspects emanating from
signs (a peculiarity to Parasari system), effects of sub planets
(a great discovery by Indian astrologers), a number of
systems relating to dasa periods, Ashtaka Varga, exclusive
tules for nativities of the fair sex, methods to locate lost birth
data, planetary rays, effects of births at eclipses, New Moon
etc.. planetary locations in various houses and many such
other valuable information are full in this work.

The present version runs voluminously into 97 chapters
and hence is byfar the largest edition.

The work is translated in English by R. Santhanam by
whom no stone is left unturned in enlightening every difficult
stanza with clarifications. This is a widely acclaimed treatise
= is most sought after by the students and savants of astro-
ogy.
book_name: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)

chunk_id: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)_p13
text:
9
PRASNA MARGA

This eternal classic occupies a high position of pride in
the chrest of the peacock of Hindu Astrology. Just as one
cannot take pleasure at the peacock’s chrest unless it is thrown
wide open, the riches of our astrology will not be discovered
without entering in the mines of works like Prasna Marga.
The farther one is from such literature, the darker the secrets
ofthis super science. Prasna Marga took its birth through
an extremely intelligent astrologer of Kerala during the 16th
century. ;

The author presents the short and the long of many
standard traditional astra] literature of those days including
KRISHNEEYAM. Though the name of the text looks as if
it deals with (only) Horary astrology, it does as well deal with
natal astrology, Karma, Remedial Measures etc. in great and
possible detail. A significant importance has been given to
Mantra Sastra also.

Ashta Mangala Prasna, a peculiarity which has been
successfully encashed by Kerala astrologers, can be found
exclusively in Keralite literature like the present work. Ina
way, Ashta Mangala Prasna is Hindu numerology. Exhaustive
chapters are devoted to diseases, omens, Trisphutas, Kala-
chakra Dasa, spirits, matrimony rains, silent questions (Mooka
Prasna—a system to know what the query is when untold),
dreams, Ashtaka Varga etc. All the chapters have received
scholarly treatment in the hands of the author. The
32 chapters are thus divided in 3 volumes with easily
understandable English translation and commentary by
Sri J.N. Bhasin and in Hindi by Sri Sukdev Chaturvedi.

JATAKA PARIJATA

The reader will be convinced that the work in question
is among the most essential ones if one aims at perfect mastery.

This work is one of the greatest classics in the series of
of classical astrology we inherit from our pastmasters. The
author is Venkatesa Daivagna’s son, Vaidyanatha. The former
authored Sarvartha Chintamani which is also full of instructive
and suggestive rules on predictive astrology. Vaidyanatha in
his text gives the quintessence of Garga Hora, Parasara Hora,
book_name: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)

chunk_id: Saravali of Kalyana Varma Santhanam R. (Astrology)_p14
text:
10

Brihat Jataka, Hora Sara, Sarvartha Chintamani etc. and has
covered many sections on horoscopy in a systematic and lucid
manner, in as many as 1900 slokas in Sanskrit.

Apart from covering elementary aspects for a student’s
easy grasp, the text deals with the properties of each zodiacal
sign and of the planets, their nature, combinations to curtail
or bestowing good longevity, combinations of planets to mar
a nativity, those that confer position. effects of two and more
planets, effects of Gulika, effects of births in the sixty Jovian
years (Brahaspathya Varsha), effects of births in various asteri-
sms, details of results of Ashtaka Vargas, the 12 houses, female
horoscopy, and various kinds of planetary periods. The great-
ness of this work lies in each and every chapter.

The English translator, late Pandita Vidyabhushana
Subramanya Sastri gives simple account with notes and
examples. Students and scholars alike enjoy this work and
derive benefits galore.

SATYA JATAKA

Sage Satyacharya, reveredly quoted by Varaha Mihira,
Prithu Yasas, Kalyana Varama, Balabhadra etc.—pillars of
Hindu astrology—in their works, lays down exemplary and
scientific principles through this monumental piece on predictive
astrology. These were originally communicated by Dhruva to
Jaimini, the latter to Garga, from whom Sage Vyasa acquired
these. Sage Vyasa taught this science to Satya. Bhattotpala,
the celebrated commentator of Varaha Mihira, quotes many
of these slokas in his commentary on Brihat Jataka.

Satya Jatakam contains many unique and not-much-
known principles. Significance of planets, Pancha Siddhanta
criterions, results of house lords in various houses, rules for
study of Navamsa chart, Planetary transits, effects of planetary
periods with reference to different houses, longevity calcula-
tions, combinations for kingship and various such other relative
and reliable information that goto benefit an astrologer are
summarized in this work.

The English translation is enriched by an addition of
important Sanskrit slokas (with English translation) from
Bhattotpala’s commentary on Brihat Jataka as well as Chinta-
mani commentary. The ultimate beneficiary is the reader who
ts always in search of Truth.

text:
14

GARGA HORA
By Sage Garga (English Edition)

Sage, Garga, an offspring of Brahma, the Creator ०
Universe, composed various astrological principles through
his divine insight. Just as the planetary indications never err,
60 also the words of the sages.

Sage Garga has deviced exclusive principles dealing with
results of planetary combinations of all possible permutations.
These relate to the 12 houses in groups of planets ranging from
two to seven. Other very useful clues to aspects of predicative
astrology are also discussed in this old classic. Atotal of
over 1400 principles are found in this work.

The English edition is prepared by R. Santhanam with
suitable notes and clarifications. This work is a must for every
student wanting to possess authoritative information.

NASHTA JATAKAM
By Achary Mukanda Drivagna
(Sanskrit-English)

Tracing unrecorded date of birth and time is no more a
problem. The author, Mukunda Daivagna bas enunciated a
great number of fool-proof methods after a study of the
schools of Varaha Mibira, Kalyana Varma and others. Many ~
such rules have been well-developed by him and are taught
to the student in an explicit manner. The Kerala system of
Lost Horoscopy in the 3rd chapter paves 22 different ways, to
reach the route of the riddle, i.e. working out the year of
birth, planetary season, month, thithi, nakshatra, ascendant,
navamsa etc. and wonderfully enough even the planetary
positions at birth.

Many new rectification methods are added in the fourth
chapter to help one arrive at a doubtless horoscope. A
number of people achieved success in knowing their lost:
horoscopes with the help of this first ever published and unique
monumental.

The English translation is by R. Santhanam, who has.
added exhaustive appendix with much fmore relevant infor-
mation in this direction. This appendix deals, inter alia, with
effects of births in the 108 navamsas and additional classical
methods to trace a lost-horoscope.

1. Glory to the Sun God, whose very breathing causes
Creation in this world, with whose rising all the animals
(living beings) wake up (from the sleep of their ignorance),
whose state atthe head (i.e. zenith) accentuates all activities
(throws the brightest light on one’s wisdom) and whose setting
(absence or weakness) causes all to sleep. Such Sun God has his
efficacy spread (all over).

Notes: Most of the astrological savants in recognition

of the importance of the Sun, praise him in the beginning of
their works. Kalyana Varma, the king-astrologer, throws
light on the Sun’s importance in all daily walks of life. The

Sun’s rise releases one of his night sleep and imparts’“‘creation”’
‘in his daily activities. Similarly, ifthe Sun, at birth, is on the
east, he bestows creative power on the native. When the Sun

reaches the head at noon, human activities are at height.

Likewise, when he is in the Zenith in the horoscope of birth

(i.e. om the 10th cusp), one is likely to reach enviable heights

5-6. THE LIMBS OF KALA PURUSHA AND THEIR USE :
The signs Aries etc. represent the limbs of Kalapurusha as
under :

Aries : head; Taurus: face; Gemini : arms; Cancer:
heart; Leo: stomach; Virgo: hip; Libra: space below navel
and private parts; Scorpio: private parts; Sagittarius: thighs;
‘Capricorn : knees; Aquarius: ankles; Pisces: feet. If at birth,
one has benefic planet in a particular sign, the particular limb
will be strong. A malefic makes such a limb weak.

, Notes: While for Kalapurusha, the respective limbs are
identified from Aries onwards, for an individual, apart from
considering the Rasis as above, one should count from the
natal lagna also. For example, suppose one+is born in Leo,
This sign indicates his head. Hence, when assessing the head of
a Leo native, look at Leo and Aries as well.

text:
१ 24 Saravali

Notes : How to calculate the Navamsa positions is
explained below :

Each Rasi has nine Navamsas of 3°-20' each, The coun-
ting for Aries, Leo and Sagittarius is done from Aries itself.
It is done from Capricorn for the signs Taurus, Virgo and
Capricorn. For Gemini, Libra and Aquarius, the counting is
commenced from Libra. The Navamsas of Cancer, Scorpio and
Pisces range from Cancer.

Another method is to count from stellar quarters. Take
the first 3 stars or 12 quarters and count from Aries and con-
clude with Pisces. Proceed in the same manner for each group
of 3 stars again and again.

That is, Aswini-Bharani-Krittika form one group the

. Navamsas of which range from Aries to Pisces. Then again

proceed in the same way for Rohini-Mrigasira-Aridra group
and for other groups.

16. LORDS OF SAPTAMSNS : The Saptamsas for the
12 Rasis from Aries onwards are respectively counted from
Aries, Scorpio, Gemini, Capricorn, Leo, Pisces, Libra, Taurus,
Sagittarius, Cancer, Aquarius and Virgo. The lords thereof
-are those in whose Rasis they fall.

Notes : The idea is that the counting of Saptamsa for
odd signs starts from that sign itself. In the case of even Rasis,
it is from the opposite house. For example, the counting of
1/7th Amsa starts from Leo itself for Leo, the second is Virgo,
third is Libra and soon and so forth. For Cancer, an even
Rasi, the counting is done from Capricorn. Each Rasi is
made in seven equal parts to be called as Saptamsa. Each
Saptamsa thus equals to 4°17’ 837.

Till now in the previous pages, we have had the following
divisions :

1. Rasi 2. Hora 3. Drekkana 4, Navamsa 5. Dwa-
dasamsa 6.Trimsamsa 7. Saptamamsa. There are other
mine essential divisions to complete the Shodasa Varga (16
Varga) scheme. Some are satisfied with 10 Vargas and yet

ending in Pisces. Similarly, for other signs, suitable deductions
can be made.

(12) .Chaturvimsamsa: Each Rasi is madein 24 equal
parts. This is also called Siddhamsa. One such division is
made up of 1° 15’ of arc. For odd signs, the 24 divisions are
counted from Leo while for even signs the counting is from
Cancer. For example, the first Siddhamsa of Aries is Leo,
the 2nd is Virgo, the 3rd is Libra, the 13this again Leo, the
14th is Virgo while the 24th one is Cancer. In the case of
Taurus, an even sign, the Ist one is Cancer, the 2nd one is
Leo, the 3rd one is Virgo, the 12th one is Gemini, the 13th
is again Cancer, the 14th is again Leo while the 24th is again
Gemini. The intermediary ones may be suitably understood
accordingly.

(13) Nakshatramsa or Sapthavimsamsa: This is 1/27th
part of a Rasi. The counting starts from the Rasi itself,
whether it is an odd one or an even one. For example, for
Aries, start from Aries and count upto Pisces, you get 12
Sapthavimsamsas. Then the 13th one is again Aries, the 14th is
Taurus, the 24th is Pisces, the 25th is again Aries, the 26th one
is Taurus and the 27th one is Gemini.

(14) Swa-Vedamsa : Each Rasi is made in 40 equal parts,
each of 45’ of arc. The counting for odd signs commences
from Aries while Libra is the starting point for even signs.
For example, for odd Rasis, we complete 3 rounds from Aries
to Pisces to get 36 Swavedamsas. The 37th one is again Aries,
the 38th one is Taurus, the 39th one is Gemini and the 40th
one is Cancer.. Similarly for even Rasis, starting from Libra,
we complete 3 rounds upto Virgo to get 36 Swavedamsas. The
37th, 38th, 39th, and the 40th ones are respectively Libra,
Scorpio, Sagittarius and Capricorn.

(15) Akshavedamsas : Each Rasi is divided into 45 equal
parts, each of 40’ ofarc. In the case of movable Rasis, the
rulership is counted from Aries; from Leo in so far as fixed
Rasis are concerned and from Sagittarius for dual signs,

Saptavargas : These consist of Rasi, Hora, Decanat e,

The use of the said 16 vargas is explained below :

(4) Turyamsa Bhagya or prosperity

(5) Sapthamsa Children and grand children
(6) Navamsa Wife

(7) Dasamsa Profession
(8) Dvadasamsa Father and mother

(9) Khalamsa Conveyances

(10) Vimsamsa Success

(11) Chaturvimsamsa Education

(12) Nakshatramsa Strength

(13) Trimsamsa Malefic results

(14) Swavedamsa Good and,.bad

(15) Akshavedamsa Good and bad

(16) Shashtiamsa Good and bad

nates is sixty (24-+36), inthe entire Zodiac. The figure is
increased by 72 if the total of Horas+Navamsas is required,
ie. 60-+-72=24+4-108=132. Each Rasi has 1800 Kalas (30
degrees each of 60’ of arc).

लग्नादीनां लिप्ता Rat: स्वगुहादिवग्गंसंगुरिएताः ।
अ्र्ञनादशशतभक्‍ता लब्धः  स्थादीप्सितो वर्ग: ॥१८॥

- 18. DIFFERENT VARGAS : To know any kind of Varga
(i.e. Hora, Navamsa, Drekkana etc.) adopt the following
method : convert the longitude into minutes of arc and multi-
ply by the Varga figure concerned. Divide the product by
1800. The resultant figure will reveal the required Varga.

Notes : Suppose a planet is in 15° of Aries and we want
to know the Navamsa position. 15° converted into minutes
becomes 900 This should be multiplied by 9 which is the
fiigure for Navamsa. We get 8100. Divided by 1800, this
yields 4 completed Navamsas with a remainder which indicates
the 5th Navamsa. The factor of multiplication for Hora is 2,
for decanate 3, for Dasamsa 10 and so on and so forth. These
Vargas can be found from the tables already given earlier
without undergoing this ordalium.

20-21. RASIS—BENEFIC AND MALEFIC IN NATURE;
GANDANTA AND ITS EFFECTS : From Aries onwards, alter-
natively the Rasis are known as malefic and benefic on the one
hand, and male and female on the other band. These are also
classified as chara (Movable), Sthira (Fixed or Immovable) and
Dviswabhava (Ubhaya, dual orcommon) Rasis. The ending
portions of Cancer, Scorpio and Pisces are called Gandanta.
It is said that one born in Gandanta will not survive. He will
either lose his mother or he will end the dynasty, ise. he is
the last of his descent and will not have any children. Jf,

22. DIRECTIONS OF RASIS : The four Rasis from
Aries onwards indicate East, South, West and North, while
the remaining Rasis repeat in the same way. A journey under-
taken by a person towards the direction indicated by the Lagna
or the Moon (at the commencement of journey) yields fruitful
results.

Notes: According to the above rule, for example, if
Aries is the Lagna at the commencement ofa journey, or if
the Moon be in Aries, there is success in the eastern direction.

Now, the directions indicated in this Sloka are:

Aries, Leo and Sagittarius East
Taurus, Virgo and Capricorn South
Gemini, Libra and Aquarius West

Cancer, Scorpio and Pisces North

text:

Human signs are: Gemini, Virgo, Libra, Aquarius and |
first half of Sagittarius.

Quadruped signs are: Aries, Taurus, Leo, second half of
Sagittarius and first half of Capricorn.

Watery signs are: Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces and second
half of Capricorn. .

Keeta Rasi or insect sign in Scorpio as per Garga and’
Mantreswara. But Parasara includes Cancer also. Horasara
classifies the 3 watery signs as Keeta Rasi with particular
emphasis on Scorpio. Now, the first part of the sloka can
be well-conceived. Human signs are strong in the east or
ascendant. Biped signs are strong in south or the 10th.
Scorpio is strong in the West or the 7th while watery signs
(excluding Scorpio) have strength in the north or the 4th.
Similar view is found in Horasara of Prithuyasas (ch. I, s.12)
and Jataka Parijatha (ch. I, S. 16-18). The Kalabala of Rasis
are clear, as given in the text.

24, DAY AND NIGHT SIGNS—SIRSHODAYA AND
PRISHTODAYA SIGNS: Gemini, Cancer, Capricorn Aries,
Taurus and Sagittarius are nignt signs. Leo, Libra, Scorpio,
Aquarius, Pisces and Virgo are day signs. The signs Cancer,
1 Capricorn, Aries, Sagittarius and Taurus are called Prishtodaya
which rise with hind part. Sirodaya signs (rising with head)
are: Leo, Libra, Scorpio, Virgo, Aquarius and Gemini. The
sign Pisces is Ubhayodaya (i.e. that rises with both head and
hind part).

28-31 ADDITIONAL SYNONYMS.

The 4th (Vesma) and the 8th are called as Chaturasra
houses. The 9th is known as Tapa (तप) i.e. penance. The 4th
has names like Sukha (happiness), Jala (water), Patala (nether-
world), Bandhu (relatives) and Hibuka. The other names given
to the 10th house are : Karma (deed), Agna (command), Gagana
(sky or zenith) and Meshurana. The Sth and 9th are trines. The
5th is the only house connected with Dhi (i.e intellect) while the
9th house is Tritrikona, ie. the trine of trine. The 7thh ouse is
called Dyuna, Astha (setting), Jaayg(sTat) and Jamitra. The 6th
is Shatkona; the 3rd is Duschikya and the 12th Rippha. Kutumba
is the 2nd house.

Notes : The word Jamitram (जामित्र) 15 derived from जाया
and is indicative of the future goodluck of one’s wife. The
word is obviously connected with the Greek word diametron.

Regarding trines, only the 5th and the 9th are strictly
trines while the ascendant is not included in this classification.

Also refer to Jataka Parijata (Ch.1), Jataka Tatwa (1-27),
Lagna Chandrika (1-6), Brihat Jataka (Ch. 1), Phal Deepika
(1-17/18) and my English translations of Brihat Parsara Hora

may know that the ascendant is not designated as a trine in any
standard astral literature.

32. KENDRA, APOKLIMA AND PANAPHARA-
EFFECTS-THEREOF : The adjoining houses with reference
to angles (Kendras) are Panaphara. The houses next to
Panapharas are Apoklima houses, The planets in angles give
effects in one’s boyhood. The effects of planets in Panapharas
are felt in the middle age while the planets in Apoklima give
result at the conclusion of the natives’s life.

Notes : Kendras are Ist, 4th, 7th and 10th houses. Pana-
pharas (also called cadent houses) are the 2nd, 5th, 8th and
11th. Apoklima houses (succedent) are the 3rd, 6th, 9th and
12th houses. The good or bad effects are specially revealed in
the boyhood, youth and old age according to their placement
in angles, cadents or succedents.

37-38. LONG, MEDIUM AND SHORT ASCENSIONS
OF SIGNS ; Pisces, Taurus, Aries and Aquarius are signs of
short ascension. Gemini, Sagittarius, Cancer, and Capricorn
are of medium ascension. The long ascension signs are:
Scorpio, Virgo, Leo and Libra. The head and other organs of
the native will be similar to the sign ascending at birth and
other relative signs. The planets occupying such houses will
also influence the form and shape of the various organs.

| Notes : If, for example, Scorpio ascends at birth, the head
of the native will be long. Should Aries rise, the head is small.
The influence lent by planets will also have a say on this
matter. For significations of organs, see next chapter.

In Jataka Parijatha, Ch. 1, Pisces is stated to be of
medium ascension.

Also see Horasara, Ch. I, sloka 24.

king undertakes his journey towards such direction indicated
by the Rasi concerned, he attains quick suceess.

22. DESCRIPTION AND NATURE OF THE MOON:
She has an auspicious appearance. Her eyes are beautiful.
She has mellifluous voice. She is fair in complexion, has an
emaciataed body, is youthful, tall, curly and short hair, is learn-
ed, soft, of Satwik disposition, beautiful, phlegmatic, amicable
to friends, has pure (excellent) blood, is kind, fond of
old Jadies, fickleminded, very fortunate, and wears clean
apparels.

Notes : The Moon is a cold body in the planetary
scheme, is black in complexion (इयामस्तनु), has a round body,
is windy and phlegmatic, peaceful, soft-spoken, auspicious
in sight, indicates queen, mother, fare, water-born products,
mind, flowers, salt, palatable articles, lead, pearl, zinc, ram etc,
She drinks milk and water : (Ref. Poorva Purasaryam).

The Moon does not have much hair. She is more phle-
gmatic than windy. She hasa mix of both old age and youth,
(Phala Deepika).

23. DESCRIPTION AND NATURE OF MARS : The
planet Mars is short; his eyes are reddish-brown in colour.
He has a strong body. He is as bright as burning fire. He is
not steady in disposition. He has marrow in bones and flesh.
He wears red-coloured apparels. He is more intelligent arid
is courageous. He is an accomplished speaker (निष्पन्तनवाक ).
He causes injury. He has short and shining hair, He is bilious
in disposition and is Tamasic. He is adventurous and wrath-
ful and is skilful in hurting. He is blood-red in appearance.

24, DESCRIPTION AND NATURE OF MERCURY :s
Mercury’s eyes are reddish and broad. He speaks sweetly. Hi
complexion is of green grass. He has strong skin. He is an
extremely Rajasic planet. He is clear in his spcech and is pure.
He has a mixture of the three humours, i.e phlegm, bile and
wind. He remains delighted and has moderate beauty. He is
skilful, and sinewy. He is agreeable to all in dressing and speech
and wears green robes.

Notes : Mercury is learned, emaciated, Vaisya (business

ercial planet. He is young and isa mathematician. His dis-

grand-mother, friendship, relatives, heart, pearloyster, Rajasic
disposition, delirium fever, forgetfulness, diseases of various
kinds and mixed eatables. He rules skin and has reddish, large
eyes. He is of medium stature, sometimes firm and sometimes

25. DESCRIPTION AND NATURE OF JUPITER:
The eyes of Jupiter are somewhat reddish-brown. He is learned
in Vedas. His voice resembles that of a lion. He is a firm and
is prominently Satwic. His physical complexion is akin to pure
yellow metal. He has broad and prominent chest. He is always
fond of virtues and is modest. He has firm and large eyes
and is of forgiving disposition. He wears yellow apparels, is
phlegmatic, fat and pre-eminent.

Notes : “बद्धोत्कटाक्ष” has been interpretediby some comm-
entators as “‘wearing excellent rosaries”. This view cannot be
subscribed to, while I have rendered the same as having firm
and large eyes. See gg=firm, उत्कट--14186 and 7e— eyes.

Jupiter has large eyes and hence his aspect on or occup-
tion of the 2nd house confers large and beautiful eyes.

He has large belly. He is a minister, has sharp mind,
and is gold-coloured. He denotes abdomen, wisdom, work,
hearing and auspiciousness. He consumes honey, ghee and.
Sweet food. He is virtuous and meritorious and is an expon-
ent of mythology. He indicates happiness, Vedic rituals,
Sacrifices etc. He rules Brahmins, sages and ascetics and
possesses empirical disposition. (Vide Poorva Parasaryam).

27. DESCRIPTION AND NATURE OF SATURN :
Saturn has red and depressed eyes, emaciated body, prominent
veins, is indolent, black-bodied, and windy in temparament.
He is a tale-bearer. He is muscular, unkind, foolish, has large
nails and teeth, is very dirty, impure, Tamasic in disposition,
fierce, short-tempered and old (worn out). He adores black
apparels.

Notes: He is cruel, lame and is indicator of misery,
longevity, livelihood, death, disputes, windy diseases, indolence,
devils, base men, uatouchables, sins, and legs. He is a eunuch.

The present verse is taken verbatin by Mantreswara for
his Phala Deepika, (except for minor replacement), vide Ch. I,
sloka 14.

31. FIVE KINDS OF CONSIDERATIONS OF RELA-
TIONSHIP : (a) Should there be temporary friensdhip and
natural friendship between two planets, then they become
extremely friendly (afafya). (b) If there is natural neutrality
and temporary friendship, there exists only friendship between
two planets. (c) If there is natural enmity and temporary
friendship, they are neutral in relationship. (d) Should they
be naturally neutral and temporarily inimical, they are only
inimical. (e) If there is enmity both naturally and tempora-
rily, then they are greatly inimical to each other.

हि Notes: These rules are simple and need no clarification.
However, an example is given thus: Suppose the Sun is in
Taurus and Jupiter is in Cancer. They are mutually in 3rd
and 11th. Hence, they are temporary friends in the nativity.
They are by nature friendly to each other. Hence on account
of these two sources of friendship, they become mutually
extreme friends as far as the concerned horoscope is concerned.
Now, see point (6) above. The Sun and Saturn are mutually
inimical. If these two are mutually in 6/8 positions in a chart,
they act in a highly inimical manner for each other.

2-4, AVASTHAS OR STATES OF PLANETS: Planets
have nine states of being, viz. Deepta, Svastha, Mudita, Santa,
Sakta, Nipeedita, Bhita, Vikala and Khala, A planet in its exal-
tation is said to be in Deeptavastha, In its own house, it is in
Svasthavastha, friendly house -Muditavastha, in beneficial
Vargas Santavastha, with bright rays Saktavastha, in combus-
tion Vikalavastha, if defeated in planetary war Nipeedita, in
malefic Vargas Khalavastha, and if in fall in Bhitavastha.

Notes : The indications of the above mentioned Avasthas
are given below :

Deepta (दीप्त = radiant
Svastha (taza) = confident
Mudita (मुदित = rejoiced
Santa aa) = peaceful

Sakta (34a) = strong

5. EFFECTS OF A RADIANT PLANET: Should a
planet be in Deepta state (radiant) at birth the native burns his
enemies with the fire of his valour. He gathers all kinds of
wealth. His (band of) elephants soak the surface of the earth
with the juice or ichor exuding from their temples, (That is, the
native will own a large number of elephants. See the simulta-
neous literary ability of Kalyana Varma. A number of such
comparisons can be found in this work.)

Note : Please see notes under sloka 13 of this chapter for
more information.

Should a planet be in Khalavastha (i.e. in various malefic divi-

all his wealth, will have an impure mind and be never free
from miseries.

among relatives, learning and royal honours (Sarvartha Chinta-
mani, Ch. 13).

14. RETROGRESSION IN EXALTATION: If a
planet is retrograde while in exaltation, it produces no effects
(i.e. nothing good will happen). This is the view held by some.
Even if itis in exaltation by its entry in retrograde motion
from the next sign, (for example, Jupiter entering Cancer from
Leo), the effect is same, for it takes unduly long time in such
traversion.

Notes : yfaag indicates a planet (i.e. from Mars to

Saturn) entering the previous sign while in its (R) motion, for
example from Taurus to Aries.

Many writers hold the view that a planet if be retrograde
while in exaltation loses its potence and conversely if in fall
while in its retrograde motion, it acquires strength and acts
favourably.

15. DIFFERENT EFFECTS: A planet in exaltation is
considered highly strong. It is medium in strength in its
Mulatrikona Rasi or own house. Its strength is only nominal
if it be aspected by a friendly planet or be in a friendly Rasi.

Notes : The idea behind the suggestion is that a planet in
a friendly Rasi or with a friendly planet’s aspect is not superior
to the one in its Moolatrikona Rasi while the one occupying
its Moolatrikona Rasi is second in strength to the planet that
is in its exaltation.

16-17. The elders among Yavanas say that the Moon is
of medium strength for ten days commencing from Sukla
Padyami, i.e. first day of Bright Lunar Half. During the
following ten days (i.e. from the llth day or Ekadasi of
Bright Lunar Half through the 5th day or Panchami of Dark
Lunar Half or Krishna Paksha), she is exceedingly strong.
And during the third ten-day period (ic. from the 6th day or
Shashti of Dark Lunar Half through the New Moon day or
Amavasya) she has very little strength. If at birth the Moon
has brilliant rays, is bright circled and full, it makes the native
an unencounterable king

Notes: yazq—bright. ausq—= circle or disc of the
Sun/Moon, and hence it is ‘“‘bright-circled’’.

This sloka highlights the importance of full Moon ina
horoscope.

18. GOOD RESULTS IN MID-LIFE: If at birth the
lord of Moon sign or the lord of the ascendant or Jupiter be in
Kendra (angle), the native is happy in the middle of his life.

Notes: Prithuyasas in Horasara states that if the lord of
the Moon sign or the lord of the ascendant be in angle along
with Jupiter, the native becomes happy in the middle of his
life (See Ch. 16, sloka 14). Kalyana Varma stipulates that
Jupiter in angle singly is enough to cause the said results.

25. EFFECTS OF EXALTED PLANETS ETC.: If a
planet is exalted, it gives auspicious results, to full extent. It
is futile if in debilitation. The results are limited in an enemy’s
camp. The auspicious effects given by a planet are 1/4thina
friendly house, 4 in own house and 3/4th in Mulatrikona Rasi.

Notes: The effects revealed by a planet are: 25% of its
full capacity if in a friendly house, 50% in own house, 75% in
Mulatrikona and 100% in exaltation house. In enemy’s camp,
it is less than 25%. Thus its status gradually increases as under :
Enemy’s house, friendly camp, own house or Swakshetra,
Mulatrikona and exaltation Rasi. These positions become
better in the ascending order. While in debilitation, it gives
no auspicious effects.

Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga, is however a different aspect,
and should not be handy for all practical purposes.

26. INAUSPICIOUSNESS OF PLANETS: A planet is
fully inauspicious if it is in its debilitation; it is somewhat
better in inimical house (than in debilitation). Its inauspicious
effects are nil if it is exalted, 3/4th in friendly house, 4 in own
house and 4th in Mulatrikona Rasi.

Notes: This is a converse process while the previous
sloka explains auspicious results. The inauspicious effects are
100% in debilitation, somewhat less if in inimical Rasi, nil if
exalted, 75% in friendly Rasi, 50% in own house and 25% in
Mulatrikona. Draw a comparison as under :

Position Good Bad
Debilitation Nil 100%
Inimical limited न better than fall
Friendly Day, 15%
Swakshetra 50% 50%
Mulatrikona UWA 25%
Exaltation 100% nil

For example, from the two slokas in question; we can
infer that if a Swakshetra planet can be auspicious to the extent
of 50%, a planet in friendly house can give good effects only
to the tune of 25%.

28-30. EFFECTS ACCORDING TO EXALTATION
ETC.: A planet in exaltation gives all kinds of wealth. It
confers ministership or leadership over army, if in Mulatrikona
Rasi. If it is in own house, it gives happiness, money, grains,
wealth etc. In a friendly house, it gives fame, splendour,
abundant good fortune (श्रतिस्‌ भग॑), long-lasting wealth and also
wealth through king. A planet that is in its own hora makes
one courageous.

Notes: The word “Hora” in sloka 30 is generally
interpreted as house so that it applies to all the seven planets.
But, in this context, in sloka 29, ‘‘own 10056” has already
been discussed and hence, the word “Hora” in sloka 30 means
“half”? i.e. Iunar or solar hora. Thus, the last line of sloka 30 -
should be applied only to the position of the Sun and that of
the Moon. This is the strict translation aspect, while in
practical aspect, “10147 can mean house and a person can be
valorous if a planet is in own house (or if the luminaries are
in their own hora).

34-35, If a planet is with ojha or Yugma Bala (i.e. strength
by being in male or female Rasis), the native is worshipped by
the public, expert in arts; pleased, immune to sickness and
prone to fear of other worlds. A planet with positional
strength gives perennial happiness, friendship, courage, fixity
of mind and independent profession or work.

Notes: The Moon and Venus get each half rupa by
staying in an even Rasi. Another half rupais obtained by
their stay in even Navamsa. Should they be in odd Rasi or
Navamsa, their score is nil on this count. Similarly, odd
Rasis and odd Navamsas give half rupa each, for the rest of the
5 planets. This strength is designated as ojhayugma Rasyamsa
bala (or strength by odd/even Rasi and Navamsa position) as
shown under the heading positional strength, vide notes for
sloka 35, Ch. IV.

36-38. A planet with directional strength takes the
native to the direction ruled by it and gives clothes, jewels,
conveyances etc. And the native is happy in such a direction.
A planet with Ayana Bala similarly takes t'e native to its
direction and confers various kinds of wealth apart from fame.
In both the cases, the planets should not be combust or
debilitated. If a planet has Cheshta Bala, it sometimes gives
kingdom, sometimes respect; sometimes money and yet some-
times increases the fame of the native.

Notes: We have already explained Digbala or direc-
tional strength, vide notes under sloka 35 of Chapter 4. Suppose,
Saturn is in the 7th, without combustion or debilitation, then
it takes one towards west (see sloka 8, Ch.4) and gives success.
Similarly, Ayana Bala and Cheshta Bala are explained in
slokas 36-37 of the previous chapter.

39, EFFECTS OF RETROGRADE PLANETS : A benefic
if retrograde is strong and is capable of conferring kingdom.
If a malefic is retrograde, it causes grief and purposeless
wandering.

Note ; According to Bhavartha Ratnakara, a malefic does
good if in retrogression, see sloka 16, Ch. 13. But Jataka Tatwa
(A-71) attributes strength equal to exaltation to all such
planets irrespective of being malefic or benefic.

47-50. PLANETARY STAGES (दशा ० प्रवस्था) AND
EFFECTS THEREOF: It is boyhood (बाल) if a planet isin
its own house or friendly house. In Mulatrikona Rasi, it is
in youth (कुमार) and in adolescence (युबति) in exaltation.
It is said to be in old stage (वृद्धावस्था) if it occupies an inimical
house. A planet in debilitation is in extremis (मरणण). The
corresponding effects are felt in the respective planetary dasas
i.e. periods. The results for such stages are respectively
happiness, virtue, kingdom, sickness with debts, and death or
expenses.

Notes : The दशा or ग्रवस्था explained above are by virtue
of a planet’s occupying different Rasis. Jataka Tatwa (5-68

and 69) states a different rule. In odd signs, according to the
longitude obtained by the planet, itis in infancy, boyhood,

be made in 5 parts of 6° each. This order is reverse in the
case of even signs, i.e. ‘‘infancy” isat the end, preceded by
boyhood and so on and so forth. A planet in adolescent stage
is capable of giving full results (i.e. 12° 1’ to 18° of odd or even
Rasi).

51-52. EFFECTS OF PLANETS IN ODD AND EVEN

RASIS : A planet in odd Rasi with strength makes the native
courageous and a warrior. If itis weak in odd Rasi, the
native is cruel and dullwitted. A planet in even Rasi gives soft
nature, fear for quarrels, love for water, flowers and clothes,
good fortune, health and protection of own men.

1. LORD OF WEEK DAY ETC: The Sun, the Moon,
Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus and Siturn rule the week days
etc. Whichever planet becomes the lord of the first week day in
the month of Aswinain the year of Aswina, will become the
lord of month and year.

Notes : Whichever planct rules the opening week day of
the year shall be the year lord. Thatis, ifa year starts on
Monday (ruled by the Moon), the year lord is Moon. Similarly
whoever rules the first week day of a month, is designated
as lord of the month.

अब्दाधिपाइचतुर्था: FAW षष्ठास्तु कालहोरेशाः।

2-3. The fourth planet counted from the lord of the
first yearrules the second year. Similar counting of fourth
lords for the subsequent years should be made to know the
lords thereof. There are 24 horas (i.e. planetary hours) in
each day (from one Sun-rise to the next Sun-rise). The first
Hora is ruled by the lord of the week day while the subsequent
ones are ruled by the lords of the sixth week days thereof,
Thirty (Savana) days make one (Savana) month. To know the
lord of the week day in particular month, the months past
should be multiplied by 30, increased by one and divided by 7.
The remainder represents the lord of the week day counted from
the earlier week day lord.

5-6, BHAVA EFFECTS: The good effects relating to a
bhava can be better reaped if an enterprise relating to that
bhava is started inan ascendant ruled by the lord of that
bhava, or when the lord thereof is in an upachaya house, or on
the week day, Hora, year or month ruled by that bhava lord.
The good effects will be 25% by year lord, 50% by month
lord, 75% by lord of week day and 100% by hora lord.

Notes : Suppose one wants to buy a conveyance. This
aspect is ruled by the 4th lord. Let us assume it is Mercury,
Hence a hora ruled by Mercury will be 100% beneficial for that
Purpose. Alternatively an ascendant ruled by Mercury, or
when he is in 3, 6, 10 or 11 of another ascendant may also be.
useful.

94 Saravali

womb. These ten months are ruled respectively by Venus,
Mars, Jupiter, the Sun, the Moon, Saturn, Mercury, lord of
Nisheka lagna, the Moon and the Sun. The dispositions of
these lords indicate good or bad of the respective processes.

Notes : Curious readers may refer to Horasara, P. 47
for relevant information.

Balabhadra quotes Parasara as under in this context : The
semen sprinkled in the womb coagulates in the first month. In
the second month it becomesan embryo and assumes the form
of a bubble on water and the sex of the child forms viz. male,
female or eunuch. In the third month it transforms into a
sprout. In the fourth month, the limbs become prominent (well
grown) and the bearing woman develops courage if it is a male
child, assumes timidity if it is a female child and a mixed disposi-
tion if it is a neuter. And the child in the womb also reflects such
dispositions. (Mark the discerning scientific approach of our
sages made thousands of years ago, which may bea news to
even the modern medical scientists !) In the 5th month, blood
strengthens in the child’s body. Bones, hair, nails etc.are added
in the sixth month. During the 7th month, strength and comp-
lexion improves; limbs assume fullness, the child covers the
holes of its ears etc. develops emotions, has a reminiscence of
its previous birth and finds ways and means for Relief. In the
8th month, sound, touch, feeling, etc. occur to the child, and it
develops purity of blood and movements in the womb. Then
in the 9th or the 10th month, delivery takes place.

50. FRACTION OF DAY OR NIGHT FOR DELIVERY ;
According to Nisheka Lagna being a day sign or night sign,
delivery will be after so much of fraction of night or day having
passed.

Notes :. Tatkalika Lagnain astrological parlance also
means Prasna Lagna. Hence wherever Tatkalika Lagna is
translated in this chapter as Nisheka Lagna can additionally
‘mean Prasna Lagna. Such rules may also be horarily applied to.

Now coming to the sloka proper, the time of delivery
during night or day can thus be guessed. If day sign rises at
the time of query (or in Nisheka), birth will be in night time.

Ifnight sign isin Lagna at query (or in Nisheka) then
birth should be declared in day time. Suppose the 10th degree
ofa day sign ison the ascendant; it is one third of Lagna. So,
the birth should be after one third of night is over. This clue
‘can be used in working out alost horoscope or rectification of
birth time through Prasna.

Day signs are : Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Aquarius
and Pisces. Night signs are: Aries, Taurus, Gemini, Cancer,
Sagittarius and Capricorn.

The Moon’s transit as arrived at, as per previous sloka,
may lead lo the knowledge of birth time as instructed in our
present verse.

birth in day or night and the Lagna at birth. In similar manner,

and day (week day), fortnight, Muhurta month etc. should be
guessed. Such details relating to birth should first be ascer- .

tained and then predictions should be issued with reference to

Chapter 9 Conditions at Birth

I. To know the time of delivery, I have explained the
Adhana chapter. Now I explain the natal chapter as below.

शोर्षोदये विलग्ने geal प्रसवोष्न्यधोदये चररणे:।
उभयोदये च Fea: शुभदृष्टे शोभनोइन्यथा कष्टः॥ २॥

2. KIND OF DELIVERY : If natal Lagna is in Sirshodaya
Rasi, birth is with head foremost, anda Prishtodaya Rasi at
birth indicates birth with legs foremost. The birth is with hands
in such a position ifan Ubhayodaya Rasi ascends. Should
Lagna enjoy benefic aspect, delivery would have been comfor-
table; otherwise difficult.

Notes : Sirshodaya signs are: the signs that ascend with
head first. These are : Gemini, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio and

- Aquarius, Prishtodaya signs are : Aries, Taurus, Cancer, Sagi-
ttarius and Capricorn. The sign Pisces is called Ubhayodaya
Rasi.

If a Seershodaya Rasi (like Leo) rises, the child has come
out of the womb with its head first. Should a Prishtodaya
Rasi (like Cancer) be ascending, the child comes out with legs
first. It comes out with hands first if Pisces be the Lagna.

Chapter 119

20-22.If weak Moon is in the ascendant excepting Cancer,
Taurus and Aries, and is aspected by malefics the child passes
away (soon). The Moon in the 6th or 8th if aspected by male-
fics, causes death in one year, while when aspected by benefics,
she kills in the 8th year. Should the aspect be from both bene-

93-97. EXCEEDINGLY LONG LIFE : The native lives
for 108 years if the 8th house is not occupied, while ascendant
is free from malefic residence and Jupiter is in an angle. A span
of over 108 years is granted if Jupiter and Venus are in an angle,
while there are no malefics in trines and the 8th. Predict 120
years of life if Venus is in the ascendant, Jupiter is in an angle
other than the ascendant and the 8th house is not occupied by
malefics. Similar span of life is given if Jupiter is in Caneer
ascendant along with Venus or the Moon while the 8th house
is not occupied. If angles, trinesand the 8th house are not
occupied by malefics, one’s longevity is comparable to that of
Gods.

98. ELAPSED LONGEVITY : Weak Moon along with
malefics if posited in the 8th, 7th, 12th, Ist, 5th or 9th and
unaspected by benefics will only grant elapsed longevity, i.e.
immediate death.

111-114. FATEFUL DEGREES OF THE MOON: The
Moon is capable of inflicting death ifshe is in any of the
following degrees at birth: Aquarius 21°, Leo 5°, Taurus 9°,
Scorpio 23°, Aries 8°, Cancer 12°, Libra 4°, Capricorn 20°,-
Virgo 1°, Sagittarius 18°, Pisces 10° and Gemini 22°. If the
Moon is in such degrees at birth, death is indicated in the
corresponding year and the native cannot be protected by even
God Yama.

Notes: Should the Moon bein any degree as above,
death occurs in the corresponding year. For example, if the
Moon is in 23 degrees of Scorpio, the person dies at the age
of 23.

Horasara states that the fateful degrees for the Moon
from Aries onwards are 26, 12,13, 25, 24, 4, 26, 14, 13, 25,
5 and 12 and the same for Lagna are from Aries onwards: 8,
29, 22, 22, 25,14, 4, 23, 18, 20, 21 and 10, Phala Deepika
(Ch, 13) has the same view of Prithuyasas.

Jataka Tatwa (B-138) also denotes fateful degrees for the
Moon from Aries onwards respectively thus: 8,9, 23, 22, 5,
1, 4, 23, 18, 20, 21 and 10. These are death inflicting degrees in
the respective year of age.

These can be used in horary also relating to questions on
death of an ailing person.

and essentially aspected by another benefic, destroys all evils
just as gale can uproot trees. If a‘planet has a halo (in the sky)

in war (except the Sun and the Moon), when they are in con-

less longidude wins the war. For example, Jupiter and Mercury
are in Taurus at 24° 10 and 24° 29’ respectively. Jupiter is
declared successiul as he is behind Mercury.

1. SUNAPHA, ANAPHA AND DURUDAURA YOGAS :
Excepting the Sun, any other planet in the 2nd from the Moon
causes Sunapha yoga, in the 12th Anapha yoga and in both the
2nd aad the 12th Durudhura yoga.

Notes : Just as the Sun, the nodes are also not considered
in this kind of Raja yogas.

The Moon should have a planet either in the 12th or in
the 2nd (or at least with it) so that she remains balanced.
Otherwise, Kemadruma yoga is caused, as could be seen in
the following verses and notes thereof. Unless the Moon is
thus well-balanced, the native is not likely to feel comfortable
in life. He will face only obstacles in day-to-day affairs. The
said 3 yogas as per the present sloka are caused only by Mars,
Mercury, Jupiter, Venus and Saturn. Each position by a
different planet manifests varied effects, vide infra.

Ua न यदा योगाः केन्द्रग्रहरजितः शशाडूइच ।
केमद्रमोइतिकष्ट: शशिनि समस्‍्तग्रहाहष्ठे ॥२॥

2. KEMADRU MA YOGA : Kemadruma yoga is formed
if at least one of the said three yogas (viz. Sunapha, Anapha
and Durudhura yogas) is not present or if the Moon’s angles
are devoid of planets or if she is unaspected by all the planets.
Such yoga causes extreme difficulties.

Notes; Elaborate notes and comments can be found in
my English translation of Horasara (P, 230) which the reader
may consult,

6. EFFECTS OF DURUDHURA YOGA : One born
in Durudhura yoga will be famous on the earth for his speech,
wisdom, valour and virtues. He will enjoy freedom, comforts,
wealth and conveyances. He will be liberal but will come to
gtief by maintaining his family members. He will have good
behaviour.

Notes : One born with this yoga, will enjoy the comforts
generated by him, be charitable, and be endowed with wealth,
conveyances and attendants (See ch. 37 of BRIHAT
PARASARA HORA SASTRA).

According to Horasara, P. 228, such a native will enjoy
luxuries and wealth. He will be an eloquent speaker, be fond
of battles and be an army chief. He will be in a state of
bewilderness, be kingly and sacrifying in nature. He will
have cattle,

Vide Jatakabharana, the native will enjoy wealth,
conveyances, lands etc., be equal to a king and be happy with
women.

Compared to Sunapha and Anapha yogas, Durudhura
yoga is more effective as it is not that frequent to occur. The
Moon gets enormous strength to yield good effects if she is
planked by planets on both the sides. The native, according
to Parasara, is destined to experience comforts of his own.

ETC.: If Anapha yoga is caused by Mars-being in the 12th from

gives an exceedingly great liking for women. The native will
be dear to the king and will enjoy pleasures He will be
splendorous, famous and will have abundant gold. 1 Saturn
causes this yoga, the subject will be broad-shouldered, be a
leader, be inclined to keep up his commitments, be rich with
quadrupeds, devoted to an infamous lady and be virtuous.

30. LUNAR YOGAS FROM THE SUN: If the Moon
is in an angle from the Sun, 1616 effects will be felt in regard to

Notes : Should the Moon be in the 6th or 12th from the
Sua, she promotes one’s learning, wealth, wisdom etc.

३1. MOON IN VISIBLE AND INVISIBLE HALVES :
In the case of a night birth, if the Moon is with Parivesha
(halo), and be declining in the invisible half of the zodiac
(i.e. being posited between Lagna Bhava Madhya and the
centre of the descendant-—7th house), or declining Moon in day
time bein the visible half (i.e. between the centres of the
descendant andthe ascendant), even results will be felt in
respect of fear, grief etc. (That is. these positions confer bad
results). The Full Moon gives kingdom, if she be contrarily
posited.

Notes: The Full Moon in the case of day birth in the
invisible half and in the case of night birth in the visible half
confers kingdom.

33. There are other Raja yogas like Adhi yoga arising
from the Moon. I have not dealt with them here which I have
done infra.

Notes: The other yogas, namely Adhi yoga etc. are

21. JUPITER-SATURN YOGA : A native who has
Jupiter and Saturn together: at birth will be heroic, will have
plenty of wealth, will be chief of the city (mayor etc.), famous,
and will be the head of an assembly, a village or an association.

Notes : This combination is congenial for political posi-
tions, like assembly speakers, village heads, mayors etc. as
well as heads of associations etc. Such people are also found
as successful doctors and advocates.

Jatakabharana has almost similar views as of Kalyana
Varma, but adds that the native will achieve his aims.

and be owner of quadrupeds.

Notes : If these two are strong in 2 good house, one will
take to good professions described above, viz. painting, boxing
etc. Should they be weak, the native will resort to mean pro-
fessions, viz., barbour, wood-breaking etc.

position to win over the enemies.

subjected to eye-diseases, be very wealthy, be interested in
Sasthras, fables, poetry, meetings and stonework and will
become a good writer.

and Venus be together, the native will be insatiable, talkative,
intent on wandering, and subjected to grief on account of
women.

Notes : If they join in the 7th, other than in Libra or
Taurus, the marital front will be greatly disturbed. In Libra, if
the Sun is predominantly strong in Shadbala it may even deny
marriage.

३7-38. BENEFICS IN CONJUNCTION AND MALE-
FICS IN CONJUNCTION : If benefics are in conjunction at
birth, the native will enjoy wealth, sovereignty and fame. Such
a combination produces an emperor capable of ruling the earth.
Should three malefics join at birth, the native will be unlucky,
indigent, grief-stricken, ugly and immodest.

Notes : The combination of three benefics will produce
splendid results even in the 6th and 8th—not to speak of other
non-trika houses, for Adhi yoga is produced. Even in the 12th,
the conjunction of 3 planets, one of them being the 2nd lord,
causes powerful monetary yoga, (see Sarvartha Chintamani).
Malefics conjunct prove adverse and it is better that they are
separated from eaeh other. Their concentration on one parti-
cular Bhava—by way of conjunction—proves severely inauspi-
cious for that particular house while their separation distributes
the imbalances evenly.

इति कल्याणुवर्म विरचितायां सारावत्यां त्रिग्रहयोगो नाम
षोडशो5घ्याय: ॥६६॥

Thus ends the 16th Chapter entitled ‘3 planets in combi
nation” in Kalyana Varma’s Saravali.

The seven planets should only be in the said order. The
grouping can be in any number within the requirement of the

2. Sara (Ishu) yoga is formed if the seven planets restriet
themselves within the 4 houses continuously, commencing from
the 4th house, i. e. in the 4th, 5th, 6th and 7th houses.

3. Sakthi yoga forms if the seven planets are posited in
continuous four houses, commencing from the 7th through the
10th house.

4. Danda yoga is constituted if the 4 houses from the 10th
to ascendant are occupied by the seven planets.

The formation of Sara, Sakthi and Danda yogas can be
conceived from the example chart given for Yupa Yoga above,

There should not be any vacant house in between, in
these 4 yogas as well as the ones under the previous sloka.

13. ARDHA CHANDRA AND GADA YOGAS : If the
seven planets occupy cotinuously seven houses commencing
from a house which is not angular to the ascendant, the yoga
produced is known as Ardha Chandra (Ardha Sasi) yoga. Gada
yoga forms when all the seven planets occupy two succesive
angles from the ascendant.

Notes : Ardhachandra yoga should not commence
from the ascendant, 4th, 7th or 10th from ascendant. It can
start from the 2nd, 3rd, 50, 6th, 8th, 9th, 11th or 12th and
the planets should bd stationed continuously in seven houses,
For example, if this yoga starts from the 2nd house, all the
participating planets should be in the 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, 6th,
7th and 8th in continuous order.

Gada yoga is formed by all the seven planets remaining
(a) in the ascendant and 4th, or (b) in the 4th and 7th or (c)
in the 7th and 10th (d) or in the 10th and ascendant.

Thus, there are 8 kinds of Ardha Sasi yogas and 4 kinds
- - of Gada yogas.

14 VAJRA, YAVA, PADMA AND VAPI YOGAS: If
benefics occupy the Lagna and 7th while malefics are in the
4th and 10th Vajra yoga is formed. Conversely, if malefics are
in the Lagna and 7th while 4th and 10th are occupied by benefics,
Yava yoga is prcduced. If all the planets are disposed in four
kendras, Padma (Kamala) yoga is formed. Should the seven
planets be relegated either to Apoklima houses (3, 6, 9 and
12th) or to Panapharas (2, 5,8 and 11 houses), Vapi yoga is
formed.

Notes : There is a technical flaw in Vajra yoga. Malefics
ie. the Sun etc. should bein the 4th and 10th while benefics
like Mercury and Venus should be in the Lagna and 7th. There
cannot be so much elongation between the Sun and Mercury
or Sun and Venus that they are 4 houses apart. This defect
lies in Yava yoga also. That is why Varaha Mihira too raises
a query in sloka 6, ch. 12 in Brihat Jataka as to how Mercury
and Venus can be inthe 4th house from the Sun. He simply
explained Vajra, Yava and the like according to ancient
Sasthras. Gunakara in Hora Makaranda also states in this
context : qafsuga war faaat war भवेतामिति सुप्रसिद्धम्‌ |

15, SAKATA, VIHAGA, HALA AND SRINGATAKA
YOGAS: If all planets occupy only the ascendant and the 7th
house, Sakata yoga is formed. Similar participation of all the
planets in the 4th and 10th houses constitutes Vihaga (Pakshi)
Yoga. Should all the planets bein mutual trines, avoiding
the ascendant, Hala yoga is formed. All planets relegated only
to the ascendant and its trines give rise to Stringataka yoga.

This Sakata yoga, a kind of Nabhasa yoga, is different
from the usual Sakata yoga, popularly formed by the mutual
quincunx position or 2/12 position of Jupiter-Moon, se¢

text:
Chapter 21 213

Phala Deepika, ch. 6, sloka 14. The present yoga is simple and
needs no explanation. }

Vihaga (Pakshi) yoga forms when ail planets are in 4th
and 10th only. Mercury, Venus and the Sun should fall either
in the 4th or in the 10th in lot and they cannot be separated
in this yoga.

Hala yoga is formed if all the planets are in mutual
trines, but not falling in Lagna. This naturally restricts the
participation of Sth and 9th houses as well. Thus, the seven
planets sohuld be in groups as under :

(a) 2nd house and its trines, i.e. 6th and 10th, or
(b) 3rd, 7th and 11th-mutual trines, or
(c) 4th, 8th and 12th-mutual trines

Thus, there can be 3 kinds of Hala Yogas, which make
the planets remain in mutual trines.

Srinagtaka means a triangle. When all the planets are
in mutual trines a triangle is formed. In this casc, the planets
Should be in ascendant, 5th and 9th.

In Hala Yoga and Sringataka, the seven planets get
distributed to 3 different Rasis while in Sakata and Pakshi yogas,
the participant houses are 2. The trinal yogas (Hala and
Sringataka) are better than the other two, Sakata and Pakshi,
formed by opposition.

राइयन्तरितेलंगनात्‌ु. षड़भवनगतेभंवेच्चक्रम्‌ ।
ग्रर्यात्तयेव.. यातंश्चक्राकारो. BASHA: ॥ १६ ॥

16. CHAKRA AND SAMUDRA YOGAS: Should all
the planets occupy alternative Rasis commencing from the
Lagna, Chakra yoga is formed. If these occupy alternative
Rasis from the 2nd onwards, Samudra yoga is formed.

Notes : In Chakra yoga all the planets (excluding nodes)
should be in six odd houses commencing from Lagna. These
should be in six even bhavas commencing from the 2nd, to
cause Samudra yoga. The following charts will give an
illustration of these two rare but exemplary yogas /

’ 20-21. EFEECTS OF NABHASA YOGAS : NAU YOGA :
The. native’s livelihood will be through water; he will have
many gains, will be very famous, be pleased, mean, strong and
stingy. ‘This and others yogas are effective at all times indepe- .
ndant of Dasas. .

- Notes : According to Brihat Parasara Hora Sasthra (ch.
35, sloka 36), the native with this yoga, will derive his liveli-
hood, through water and become wealthy, will have many
desires, be famous, wicked, miserly and dirty. (The name at
suggests a boat and hence isa favourable yoga for business
abroad, through water, navigators, divers etc.) Varaha
Mihira in Brihat Jataka states that happiness in not uninterru-
pted with this yoga (vide ch. 12). The reason is that a boat
is not destined to remain steady in one place and moves to
and fro in many conditions.

Jataka Parijata _ explains away this yoga, stating
“नौभवस्तोय जीवी” meaning that one with Nau yoga lives
through water.

24. CHAPA YOGA (KARMUKA YOGA): The native with
Chapa yoga will be a liar, jailor, thief and will live in forests.
He will not have wealth in the middle of his life.

Notes : The results attributed for this yoga (otherwise
called Karmuka yoga) in Brihat Parasara Hora Sastra (ch 35)
are: the native will be a liar, will protect secrets, be.a thief, be
fond of wandering in forests, be devoid of luck but be happy in
the middle of his life (चुभावयोमथ्ये),

Brihat Jataka (ch. 12, sloka 16) asserts that the native
will be brave and will be happy in the beginning and end of
his 16 (प्रथमान्त्य सौख्य:).

Hora Ratna’s sloka, though verbatim taken from
Parasara, has a change at the end, reading भाग्यहीना aaa
only to fall in line with Varaha Mihira.

Jataka Parijatha gives the effects tersely thus: fond of
wandering in forests, bea theifand be blameworthy. The
native will be highly honoured and will be able to use arrows
etc. (i.e. weapons).

The short and the long Of the effects: the person with
this yoga may turn out to bea dacoit, robber etc. using guns
and such other weapons. Should malefics like Rahu, Saturn
etc. be strong, this yoga will in all probability produce a person
as above.

happy at the beginning and end of his life, bold, beautiful, free
from sickness. unfortunate, and be inimical to his own people.

Brihat Jataka states that the native will be happy in the
initial and concluding portions of life, be beautiful and valorous.

the middle of his life. He will be given to anger and be
libidinous.

36. SAMUDRA YOGA : Should a person be born
under this yoga, he will have abundant wealth and precious
stones, will be a king, be endowed with pleasures. be dear to
people, will have steady mind and be truthful in disposition
(“Satwavanta’’).

Notes : This yoga is only next to Chakra yoga,
in giving good effects. Three evil houses are involved in Samu-
dra yoga and grade of effects will depend greatly on the occu-
pants of 6th, 8th and 12th.

The effects attributed by Jatakabharana are: The person
will be charitable, courageous, virtuous, kind, will derive king’s
patronage and will make his family a fortunate one.

The native’s family will attain a supreme state with this
yoga. Note a planet should be in the 2nd in aspect to another
in the 8th. If Saturn is in the 12th while Jupiter is alsoin the
6th, the 2nd house will be influenced by both Saturn and Jupiter.

happiness, and ‘will observe religious fastings and vows. He

servations and be distinguished. This is how Brihat Parasara

possessing’ while Kalyana Varma entirely uses a different
phrase ४ आत्मनिरक्षा, Most texts have cither verbatim taken
Parasara’s slokas with some face-lift or give the short and the
long of his slokas, while particularly dealing with Nabhasa
yogas etc. as for example the present chapter. Hence, it seems
आत्मविद्‌ (to have spiritual knowledge) isa better account of the
phrase concerned.

text:
228 Saravali

41. MAALA YOGA : One born with माला योग will always
be happy, be endowed with conveyances, robes, wealth and
pleasures, splendorous and will have plurality of wives.

Notes : This falls under दल योग (Dala yoga) and
माला योग is formed when all the four angles are occupied by
benefics, excluded by malefics. This is a very rare yoga.

The present sloka is also verbatim taken by Kalyana
Varma from Parasara. The only difference is 4 in our text
which is found as qin Brihat Parasara Hora Sastra. The
practical approach remains the same in the whole context.

Varaha Mihira denotes that such a native will enjoy all
pleasures. Vaidyanatha follows the suit.

The native will be endowed with friends, sons, beautiful
ornaments, clothes, conveyances etc. He will be attached to
the fair sex for enjoyment (vide Jatakabharanam).

This yoga gives happiness and pleasures according to
Hora Makaraada. :

विषमा: az निःस्वा fied दुःखादिताः सुदीनाश्च |
परवेइ्सभक्ष्यनिरताः: सर्प जाता भवस्ति नराः॥ ४२॥

42. SARPA YOGA; The native born under this yoga
will be crooked in disposition, pitiless, always be a subject of
grief, poor and will live in other’s houses and eat their food.

Notes; This is opposite to Maala yoga and is formed
only by malefics in the four angles from the ascendant. This is
the second Dala Yoga. The occupation of only malefics in all
the 4 angles, according to Varaha Mihira is capable of causing
abundant miseries to,the native. The native will be devoid of
wealth and be sorrowful, vide Hora Makaranda. Vide Jataka-
bharana, the native with Sarpa yoga will eat other’s food, be
short-tempered, indigent, fond of sleeping, will have evil pride
and will be akin to snake when he will have to help others, i.e.
will be helpful to none.

Should only malefics occupy the four angles the evil
results attributed by the texts willcometo pass. The native
cannot make money in life, will be unhelpful to others but
depend on them and be very miserable,

43. RAJJU YOGA: The subject with this yoga will be
fond of wandering, attractive in appearance, will earn money
in foreign countries, be cruel and mischievously disposed.

Notes : Since all the planets are relegated to only mov-

live long in one place. He cannot make his livelihood in his

endowed with honour, wealth and wisdom,” be attached to his
duty, be liked by king, be famous, steady—minded and bold.

Notes -: Out of the 3 yogas, viz Rajju, Musala and Nala,

other two. As the seven planets will only be in fixed signs,
the native will have his livehood in place, will be permanently
in one place (i. ९.) be free from changes of and on), and will
retain anything earned on a lasting basis.

45. NALA YOGA : One born with नल योग will have

ctive in appearance.

Notes : In this yoga, all the four dual signs are involved
and hence the native will have mixture of previous two yogas.
Such natives may often marry more than once. If the Lagna
is also a fixed sign, two marriages are sure to follow.

1. The effects of the planets and the zodiacal signs accrue
to all and at all times. I now attempt to expiain these
according to the teachings of the rest of the preceptors.

शास्त्रा्थंक्तिकलाभि: ख्यातो युद्धप्रिय: प्रचण्डरच ।

2-3. THE SUN IN ARIES : One who has the Sun in Aries —
will comment on the meanings of Sastras, be famous in arts,
fond of war, fierce, attached to his duty, fond of roaming, will
have strong bones, be good in deeds, will do valorous acts,
will be subjected to bilious and bloody disorders, be splend-
orous and strong. The native will also become a king.

Notes: If the Sun is in Aries at birth the native will be
able to receive help from great men. He will be endowed with
abundant wisdom. The exaltaticn of the Sun gives proneness
to frequent wanderings. The word ‘Narendra’ in the text,
though translated as ‘‘king’* keeping in view the tradition. it
additionally means ‘‘curer of poisons’’. The Sun in Aries may
denote able doctors, poison-curing physicians etc. To lend
boost to monetary aspect, the Sunin Aries be better assisted
by friendly planets. This position is a good augury for political
aspirations. Jataka Jambunadeeyam adds that the Sun in Aries
indentical with ascendant gives wealth.

Coming to practical analytical aspect, the Sun in Aries is
not the same for all the 12 ascendants. He, for that matter-
any planet. gives different effects, though it be in the same
Rasi, for the various ascendants. Hence, an account of the
effects with reference to the 12 ascendants is necessary to be
known.

text:
232 Saravali

With Aries rising, the Sun in Aries causes Raja yoga;
the native achieves name and fame among the men Of his
race. Should Taurus rise with Sun in Aries, the native will be
engaged in good deeds and spends heavily. For a Gemini
nalive, the Sun in the [1 Aries, makes him rich through
brothers and bestows status in Sun’s dasa. If the Sun is in
Aries for a Cancer birth, the subject will earn huge wealth and
enjoy very high (political etc.) position. Should a Leo native
have the Sun in Aries, he will enjoy conveyances, power,
wealth etc. and will get good effects throughout Sun dasa. A
Virgo native, who has the Sunin Aries, may not get good
results in Sun’s dasa if he is conjunct with others than Mars
or Saturn. He will however spend away his money on good
account. Should the Sun bein the 7th in Aries for a Libra
native, he will enjoy a superior Raja yoga through his wife,
partnership etc. The wife may be from a very affluent race.
Should a Scorpio native have the Sun in Aries, he will lose
the whole of paternal property and face adverse -results in the
Sun’s period and will experience debacles throughout life. He
may also face loss of children. Should Sagittarius ascend at
birth with the Sun in the 5thin Aries, the native will acquire
great inheritance from father and will reach high position with
conveyances. A Capricorn native with the Sun in Aries does
not enjoy mnch of maternal happiness but a greater longevity.
He is also prone to heart diseases. One who takes birth in
Aquarius while’the Sun occupies Aries, will acquire a worthy,
chaste and forutnate wife while a native of Pisces (with the
Sun in 2nd) cannot retain any money even if he would earn a
lot. He will be subjected to physical deficiencies and diseases.

4-9. SUN IN A SIGN OF MARS IN ASPECT TO
OTHERS : Should the Sun be in a sign of Mars (i. e. either in
Aries or in Scorpio) and be aspected by the Moon, the native
will be interested in giving away gifts, will have many servants,
be charming, be dear to fair sex, and will have a soft physique.
If the said Sun is aspected by Mars, he will display his courage
in battle, be cruel, will possess eyes, hands and legs of blood-
red colour, be splendorous and strong. If the planet aspecting
is Mercury, the subject will be a servant, will do others’ jobs,
will not have much wealth, be devoid of strength, be subjected
to much grief and will prossess a dirty body. Jupiter aspecting
the said Sun denotes that the native will have plenty of
money, will donate, be a king‘s minister, a judge and a supreme
person. If Venus aspects the Sun posited in a house of Mars,
the native will be the husband of a bad women, will have many
enemies but few relatives (or not well-placed relatives), be
poor and will suffer from leprosy. In case Satura aspects the
Sun in the said condition, the native will be subjected to grief
on account of physical ailments, will have intense passion in
his undertaking, be dull-witted and a dunce.

Notes : According to Hora Ratna, the combinations
mentioned in this chapter and those upto ch. 29 hereof are
as per Satyacharya. Hence it seems to us, the word शिष्टाचार्यान्‌
in sloka I of this chapter may be a misprint that occurred
possibly in th course of communication to us and the author
Kalyana Varma might have used the word सत्याचार्यान्‌ in his
original writing. Balabhadra was a later author, who adopted
all these sloka verbatim in Hora Ratna acknowledging Saravali.
And during his time (i.e. 1654 A.D.), he might have got the
correct text.

can show a great miser. Should a Libra native have this planet

One who has the Sun in 7th in Taurus (i.c. birth in Scorpio)
will face obstacles in the celebration of marriage and will

Ifthe Sun isin the 6th for a Sagittarius birth, the native

native with Sun in 5th will be longlived but will experience
obstacles about children. The Sun in 4th for Aquarius births
will give gains through marriage and high learning. Shoulda
